title: Exploring 100 Art Styles with Illustrate Ai: The Ultimate DesignBundles Test
description:
Welcome to a unique journey where creativity meets technology! In this video, we're exploring the boundaries of artistic expression by testing 100 different art styles using the power of Illustrate Ai from DesignBundles.

Illustrate Ai, a revolutionary generative image generator, enables us to create intricate designs simply with text prompts. In this challenge, we span the breadth and depth of the art world, diving into styles ranging from classics like Realism, Impressionism, and Baroque to the edgy realms of Street Art, Cyberpunk, and Digital Glitch Art.

Experience the subtle beauty of styles like Sumi-e and the explosive color of Psychedelic Poster Art. Marvel at the fine details in styles like Medieval Illuminated Manuscripts, and enjoy the simplicity of Minimalism. Get lost in the fantastical world of Surrealism and the geometric precision of Cubism. From Abstract Expressionism to Zentangle, each style showcases the endless possibilities that Illustrate Ai offers.

Join us in th
transcript: Welcome back to another Illustrate AI tutorial.  In this video, I'll be sharing some exciting art styles you can use in your prompts to create captivating illustrations.  These styles go beyond the 18 art styles options you already have, including photographic, 3D, NMA, and more.  Here's how I tested different art styles using a basic subject and the art style digital art.  First, I selected the digital art option for testing.  Since some of these styles resemble paintings and have signatures,  and I don't want a random signature or watermark to appear on my painting,  I added the words, signature, and watermark to the negative prompt.  Then, I clicked on the Create button and waited for a minute for the images to be generated.  After that, I was able to view a larger image and publish the ones I liked.  Once the publishing process was completed, a green download button appeared.  I will begin with the prompt portrait of a cute bunny.  I used a fixed seed for consistency to observe the differences,  and then I will include a new art style each time in the prompt to see how it impacts the generation.  The default art style I will use is digital art.  I will play some music in the background so you can enjoy all the art styles.  In the top left corner, you can see the prompt I used.  Here is the image generated with Illustrate AI,  and below, you can learn more about the specific art style.  Feel free to pause it on the art style you like.  Thank you for tuning in.  If you enjoyed the video, please consider liking and subscribing to our channel for more exciting content.  If you have any questions or want to join our thriving community,  feel free to drop them in the comments below or become a part of our Pixeloma Community Group on Facebook.  Stay creative and see you in the next tutorial.
